title: Carphephorus odoratissimus
taxonomy: Carphephorus odoratissimus is classified as a species within the family Asteraceae. This plant is commonly known as fragrant oriser or fragrant carphephorus. intro: Carphephorus odoratissimus, also known as fragrant stickseed or fragrant carphephorus, is a perennial herb belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may aid in the treatment of various infections and conditions such as arthritis. Bioactive constituents found in Carphephorus odoratissimus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Carphephorus odoratissimus is often used to make tea, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues and skin conditions.
